title: There is limit of 40 statuses in status field, can this be increased as I have 40+ options to select from

posts.author: Lokesh (Lokesh Poddaturi)
posts.cookedHtml:
I use status field to select teams name to which a item belongs to, right now I have more than 40 teams and there is no way I can accommodate them.

The reason I use status field for selecting teams is

  1. It is visually easy to identify and select due to colours
  2. There are a lot many automations possible

It would be great if the limit is increased from 40 to any other possible limit.

posts.author: bottola (Lisa Botto )
posts.cookedHtml: This is a requirement for my using Monday. I have been trying to make it work and with the new GROUP BY, I need to add the clients as a status since it wont use any other column to sort by. PLEASE add more.

posts.author: Sara413 (Sara Mahan)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need this feature. We work via Kanban view and now one of my columns has reached the limit and we had to switch to a Dropdown but there is no option to group by a dropdown in Kanban, only status columns. We also can’t group by Dropdown in a table so I had to get an automation to copy the dropdown into a text column and sorted that way.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also love more Status options.

We use this as the “Date” Column and we end up not being able have all the old records do have duplicated status columns and having eg FY22/23 losing all the data from the main column.

posts.author: dwadsworth (Dustin Wadsworth)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to use a status field for US States to use in a status filter in a Workflow.

Please increase the limit here!

posts.author: patmandigital (Patrick Harman)
posts.cookedHtml: SAME! Please increase! (No response from Monday folks yet )

posts.author: paolo9785 (Paolo)
posts.cookedHtml: we have the same needs. we use it a t corporate level and if this limit won’t be increase we will be forced to move to other solutions and replace monday.com

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’re absolutely not alone here — the Status limit hits fast once you’re working at scale.

The tricky part is that the cap isn’t just about the number of colors. Status is one of the “heavy-duty” columns in monday: it powers grouping, filtering, Kanban, automations, workflow conditions, dashboards… basically half the platform depends on it. That’s why it has a hard limit right now — it’s protecting performance behind the scenes.

But your use case is 100% valid. Teams, clients, states, fiscal years… real-world lists routinely exceed 40. And with the new Group By working only on Status columns, the pressure is even higher.

Could dropdowns solve it? Almost.
If dropdowns supported:

  • color labels

  • Kanban grouping

  • Group By

  • the same automation triggers

…then yes, most people could happily move over and never look back. Color + grouping + automation parity would take a huge amount of pressure off the Status column.

Right now, though, switching to Dropdown usually means giving up the exact features you need — which is why so many folks end up stuck.

Totally hear you, totally agree this needs attention, and you’ve got a whole crowd of us cheering for the same update.

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml: Are all of the 40 statuses in use at the same time? or you’d just like them to appear as an option? if you don’t use them all at the same time, there might be a solution for this
